ops.title: tailnet https: TLS+sniff legs, SPA error panel, v0.11.1
ops.description: v0.11.0 tailnet URLs are unusable in browsers: ts.net is HSTS-preloaded so http:// MagicDNS URLs force-upgrade to https against a plain-HTTP leg (ERR_SSL_PROTOCOL_ERROR). Program (plan: ~/.cc-pool/accounts/acct-06/plans/the-native-tailscale-is-warm-badger.md): (1) synckit meshtrust.MintCert via `tailscale cert` shell-out → v0.22.0; (2) cc-present: cert manager + protocol-sniffing tailnet legs (TLS ClientHello → terminate with MagicDNS cert, else plain http — mesh clients dialing extra_addrs IPs over http must keep working) + tailnetURLs (https name URL iff cert, else http IP URL, never http name URL) + contract.md scheme rewrite + SPA ~8s timeout error panel (dead EventSource currently renders as eternal skeleton); (3) E2E across yasyf-home/yBook-Pro with dev-stamped daemon; ship v0.11.1. Separately root-caused: the reported /events 404 was a slug mangled in LLM transcription (extra char), not a daemon bug — leg serves 200 text/event-stream with the stored slug.

ops.text: Printed tailnet: URL is https://<magicdns>:<port>/... and opens live in Chrome from another tailnet machine (no cert warning, events stream connects)
Plain http to tailnet-IP legs still serves mesh clients (extra_addrs dialing unchanged)
Cert-unavailable path prints http://<tailnet-ip> URL that opens in a browser
SPA shows a visible can't-connect panel instead of eternal skeleton on a dead stream (wrong slug)
synckit + cc-present tests green (-race); v0.11.1 released with brew cask refreshed
